era_context: The late 10th century CE: The Byzantine Empire under Nikephoros II Phokas was at the height of its military power; the Fatimid Caliphate was consolidating control in North Africa; the Song Dynasty was consolidating control over China following the Five Dynasties period; the Ottonian Holy Roman Empire was expanding in Central Europe; Scandinavian Christianization was accelerating. This was an era of major political and military transformations across Eurasia.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Pre-Columbian Americas (no Old World written records)

events.title: Byzantine Military Campaigns in the Levant
events.description: The Byzantine Empire under Nikephoros II Phokas continued its aggressive military campaigns in the Levant against Islamic powers. These campaigns were part of a sustained Byzantine offensive that would continue throughout the late 10th century. Nikephoros II was one of the most militarily active Byzantine emperors, and his campaigns represented the height of Byzantine military power in the eastern Mediterranean.
events.certainty_note: Nikephoros II's broader Levantine campaigns are well-attested; specific events in 967 CE are less precisely dated. The chronology of his campaigns spans multiple years with some uncertainty in year-by-year attribution.

events.title: Song Dynasty Consolidation: Conquest of Houzhou
events.description: The Song Dynasty, under Emperor Taizu, conquered the Houzhou kingdom in the Yangtze River region. This was another step in Song reunification, bringing the Houzhou territories under Song control. The conquest was largely peaceful, with the Houzhou ruler surrendering to avoid destruction.

sources.name: Zizhi Tongjian (Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government)
Song Shi (History of the Song Dynasty)

events.certainty_note: The conquest of Houzhou is well-documented in Chinese historical records. The date 967 CE is the conventional assignment in modern scholarship.

events.title: Ottonian Expansion: Otto I's Eastern European Campaigns
events.description: Otto I, Holy Roman Emperor, continued his campaigns to expand Ottonian influence in Eastern Europe. These campaigns were part of a broader strategy to extend German and Christian influence eastward, establishing vassal relationships with Polish and other Central European rulers. Otto I's military and diplomatic campaigns laid the groundwork for the later expansion of German influence in Eastern Europe.
events.certainty_note: Otto I's broader campaigns in Eastern Europe are well-attested; specific events in 967 CE are less precisely dated. The chronology of Otto I's Eastern European campaigns spans multiple years with some uncertainty in year-by-year attribution.

events.title: Fatimid Consolidation and Preparation for Egyptian Expansion
events.description: The Fatimid Caliphate under Al-Muizz li-Din Allah was consolidating its power in North Africa and preparing for the major expansion into Egypt. The Fatimids were strengthening their military capabilities and administrative institutions in preparation for the conquest of Egypt that would occur in 969 CE.
events.certainty_note: Fatimid consolidation in North Africa is well-documented in the broader context of the late 10th century; specific events in 967 CE are less precisely dated. This entry reflects the general trajectory of Fatimid power during this period.

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of specific Byzantine campaigns in 967 CE is uncertain; the broader campaign is confirmed, but year-by-year attribution is problematic. The dating of the Song Dynasty conquest of Houzhou is sometimes placed in 966 CE or 968 CE in some sources, creating ±1 year uncertainty. The specific events attributed to Otto I's campaigns in 967 CE are less well-documented than the broader campaign trajectory.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 967 CE is dominated by Byzantine, Western European, and Chinese sources. Arab and Islamic sources are well-preserved but often compiled centuries later. Byzantine sources emphasize military campaigns; Chinese sources emphasize administrative consolidation and dynastic legitimacy. The dating of specific events in 967 CE is less precise than for later years, reflecting the general pattern of increasing documentary precision as one approaches the present. Sub-Saharan African, American, and Central Asian histories remain largely absent from contemporary written records.

graph_edges.note: Byzantine campaigns in 967 led to continued conflict in 968
Houzhou conquest is part of broader Song reunification
Otto I's 967 campaigns led to continued expansion in 968
Fatimid consolidation in 967 led to continued preparation for Egyptian expansion
